current_fact.fact_text: On 20 April 1972, Apollo 16's Lunar Module Orion touched down in the Moon's Descartes Highlands, carrying astronauts John Young and Charles Duke. It was the penultimate crewed Moon landing, chosen specifically to explore what scientists thought might be ancient volcanic terrain—only to find it was mostly crushed highland crust, rewriting ideas about the Moon’s surface.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

current_fact.match_reason: A literal shift into space and a mental 'space shift' align perfectly, with upbeat tracks echoing the bold optimism of a lunar landing.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1902, Pierre and Marie Curie succeeded in refining radium chloride, isolating the intensely radioactive substance from tons of pitchblende. The eerie blue glow and powerful rays of radium fascinated the world, opening new paths in medicine and physics while also revealing the invisible dangers of radioactivity.

episode.title: Morning Drops #42 - The Joy Of Learning
episode.description:
Hosted by Yasmin, this Morning Drop explores learning as a lifelong process, focusing on persistence, repetition, and the emotional landscape that comes with challenging oneself over time.

Through personal stories and scientific insights, the episode looks at how learning reshapes the brain, generates joy through effort, and why progress often arrives after confusion and frustration. It reflects on the so-called delayed pleasure effect, the role of dopamine in motivation, and how curiosity and learning support long-term wellbeing.

The episode also questions our cultural relationship with failure and invites a softer, more realistic approach to learning, one that accepts mistakes as part of growth and sees persistence as a quiet form of strength.

Mood: reflective, grounded, quietly hopeful
Music genre: alternative rock, hip hop, electronic, neo-soul, drill

A space to listen slowly and stay with the process.

queue.match_reason: The Curies’ painstaking refinement of radium embodies the joy and struggle of learning that this reflective, science-tinged episode explores.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1862, Louis Pasteur and Claude Bernard completed a famous swan-neck flask experiment that helped disprove the ancient idea of spontaneous generation. By showing that microbes only appeared when outside air could reach sterile broth, they revealed an invisible microbial world and transformed our understanding of life and disease.

episode.title: Morning Drops #25 - A Trip-Hop Journey
episode.description: Hosted by friend Lorenzo, a journey through the roots and evolution of trip-hop. From Bristol's early experiments with Massive Attack and Tricky, to DJ Shadow's cinematic sampling in California, and Thievery Corporation's global downtempo grooves in Washington D.C. — exploring how this genre blurred boundaries and reshaped electronic music.
songs: Tricky - Hell Is Round the Corner
DJ Shadow - Midnight in a Perfect World

queue.match_reason: Pasteur shattered an old paradigm much like trip-hop shattered genre boundaries; both reveal hidden layers beneath what we thought we knew.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1865, astronomer-priest Angelo Secchi demonstrated a simple white disk lowered into the sea to measure water clarity—now known as the Secchi disk. That humble, homemade tool became a global standard for monitoring lakes and oceans, quietly linking sailors, scientists, and climate researchers across centuries.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving, one of Gaia's great passions, and the act of immersing in life itself. A reflection on what happens when we go below the surface: when we let ourselves be fully present. From moments of calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive. An invitation to slow down, listen deeper, and float.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

queue.match_reason: A disk lowered into water to read its depths pairs with an episode about immersion and liquid, flowing sounds—both about going beneath the surface.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1861, aeronaut Thaddeus S. C. Lowe launched his gas balloon from Cincinnati and drifted an astonishing 900 miles to South Carolina in a single flight. His daring journey became an early proof-of-concept for aerial observation, inspiring the Union Army to experiment with reconnaissance balloons during the American Civil War.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

queue.match_reason: Lowe’s balloon odyssey and the episode’s ocean voyage both ride invisible currents—air or water—turning travel and navigation into a shared human adventure.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1828, French explorer René Caillié slipped into the legendary city of Timbuktu disguised as a Muslim pilgrim, becoming only the second non-Muslim to reach it—and the first to make it back alive. His quiet, low-profile journey overturned European myths of a golden metropolis and revealed a real, lived-in Saharan trading hub.

episode.title: Morning Drops #16 - The Sound of Tuareg
episode.description: A journey into the sound of the Tuareg: from traditional chants and desert rituals to the electrified rhythms of exile and resistance. Music shaped by sand, memory, and survival.

queue.match_reason: Caillié’s perilous approach to Timbuktu crosses the same Sahelian landscapes and cultures evoked by Tuareg desert blues in this episode.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1770, King Erekle II of Georgia, abandoned mid-campaign by his Russian ally Count Totleben, still managed to defeat a much larger Ottoman force at Aspindza. The battle became a symbol of a small kingdom’s stubborn resilience, wedged between empires yet unwilling to give up its independence.

episode.title: Morning Drops #36 - Women of Rock
episode.description: A tribute to the women who shaped the sound of rock, the ones who picked up a guitar and refused to ask for permission. The conversation unfolds in Italian, led by Gaia and her father. A dialogue between generations and a reminder that the pulse of rock has always belonged to anyone brave enough to play it loud. Distortion, rhythm, freedom.
songs: Joan Jett & The Blackhearts - I Love Rock 'n Roll

queue.match_reason: A king standing his ground without backup mirrors the defiant, no-permission-needed spirit of women carving out space in rock music.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1809, at the Battle of Abensberg in Bavaria, Napoleon split his forces and struck two Austrian corps in a rapid, coordinated attack. The maneuver, executed on the second day of a four-day campaign, showcased his talent for moving armies like chess pieces—and set the stage for a larger French victory.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

queue.match_reason: Napoleon’s explosive, tightly coordinated offensive fits the episode’s energy of strategic resistance and controlled musical aggression.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1861, Robert E. Lee resigned his commission in the United States Army after being offered command of Union forces, choosing instead to lead Virginia's troops. His decision, made within days, would shape the course and mythology of the American Civil War for generations.

episode.title: Morning Drops #52- Think Again
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin explores the quiet art of changing your mind, guided by Adam Grant's book Think Again. From the beliefs we carry without questioning to the identities we build around what we think we know, this episode follows the slow, sometimes uncomfortable process of unlearning.

Along the way, we meet a Serbian trumpet player who had to let go of one dream to find another, a Tunisian oud master who challenged centuries of tradition from the inside, and a Japanese band who found freedom in playing for no one. The music moves from Balkan brass to Mediterranean stillness to psychedelic drift, tracing the shape of a mind opening up.

Listen slowly, and ask yourself: is there something you believe today that deserves a second look?

Tracklist:
Boban Marković Orkestar – SAT
Anouar Brahem – The Astounding Eyes of Rita
Kikagaku Moyo – Dripping Sun
Son Lux – Lost It to Trying

queue.match_reason: Lee’s fateful choice about allegiance reflects the episode’s theme of rethinking deeply held identities and decisions, for better or worse.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1961, the Bay of Pigs invasion in Cuba collapsed as the last pockets of US-backed exiles surrendered. The failed attempt to topple Fidel Castro became a global embarrassment for the United States and pushed Cuba closer to the Soviet Union, helping set the stage for the Cuban Missile Crisis.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

queue.match_reason: A botched 'destination' in Cuba and a musical voyage called 'Destination Sedna' both hinge on where journeys lead us—and how plans can go sideways.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1949, during China’s civil war, HMS Amethyst was shelled and stranded on the Yangtze River while trying to reach the British embassy in Nanjing. The trapped frigate's months-long ordeal became a tense diplomatic incident and a story of survival under constant threat.

episode.title: Morning Drops #22 - Sicilians On The Dolomites
episode.description: Hosted by Yasmin, a musical journey inspired by the mountains. Joined by two special guests, exploring the tracks they've chosen, hearing the stories behind their picks, and discovering the personal memories each song brings to life — a blend of music, voices, and landscapes you can hear.
songs: Florian Pellissier Quintet - Coup de foudre à Thessalonique
Lucio Dalla & Francesco De Gregori - Ma come fanno i marinai

queue.match_reason: A beleaguered ship and an episode featuring "Ma come fanno i marinai" ("How do sailors manage") naturally echo each other’s maritime resilience and storytelling.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1945, as World War II neared its end, U.S. forces captured the German city of Leipzig—only to hand it over weeks later to the advancing Soviet Army under Allied agreements. The brief occupation made the city a crossroads of two soon-to-be rival superpowers at the dawn of the Cold War.

episode.title: Morning Drops #17 - Steel City
episode.description: Gaia takes us on a short trip through Sheffield's sounds — from electronic textures to raw guitars and sharp storytelling. Four tracks, one city, different edges of the same story.
songs: The Human League - The Sound Of The Crowd

queue.match_reason: Leipzig as an industrial, strategic 'steel' hub resonates with an episode about another steel city—Sheffield—where history, tension, and culture shape a city’s sound.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1945, twenty Jewish children who had been subjected to brutal medical experiments at the Neuengamme concentration camp were murdered in a school basement at Bullenhuser Damm. Their deaths remained largely hidden for decades, becoming a haunting symbol of both Nazi cruelty and the long struggle to remember individual victims.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

queue.match_reason: This solemn, reflective episode about bodily vulnerability and memory offers a respectful space to look back and honour the children lost at Bullenhuser Damm.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 2010, the Deepwater Horizon drilling rig exploded in the Gulf of Mexico, killing 11 workers and starting one of the largest marine oil spills in history. For nearly six months, crude oil gushed into the sea, blackening coastlines, poisoning wildlife, and forcing humans to confront the cost of deep-sea extraction.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

queue.match_reason: An intimate episode about life in the ocean contrasts the Deepwater disaster, highlighting what’s at stake when we damage marine ecosystems.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 2004, part of Singapore’s Nicoll Highway collapsed during underground construction works, creating a massive crater and killing four workers. The accident, one of the country’s worst civil engineering failures, led to tough new safety standards and became a case study in the hidden risks beneath growing cities.

episode.title: Morning Drops #12 - 80's Club
episode.description: Starting with a Monday evening in Munich — a few drinks, friends, and landing in an 80s dance party. From Donna Summer's hypnotic pulse, to Depeche Mode's bright synthpop, Ken Laszlo's Italo Disco romance, and Mylène Farmer's darker theatrical world. Four songs, four corners of the 80s — connected by the machines that changed how we dance.
songs: Depeche Mode - Just Can't Get Enough

queue.match_reason: The collapse of a modern, machine-shaped city artery is paired with an episode about the machines that changed how we move—both stories of infrastructure and the hidden power of engineering.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1985, activists from the Animal Liberation Front raided a laboratory at the University of California, Riverside, freeing 467 animals used in experiments and causing extensive damage. The nighttime operation shocked researchers, intensified debates over animal rights, and pushed ethical questions about how science treats living beings into the spotlight.

episode.title: Morning Drops #34 - Soul Sisters
episode.description: A tribute to four iconic voices — women who have shaped the way we listen, feel, and move. Each track carries a different kind of presence: one that holds you, one that questions, one that grounds, and one that lets go. An energy that is soulful, raw, and unapologetically feminine.
songs: Grace Jones - Walking in the Rain
Aretha Franklin - A Rose Is Still a Rose

queue.match_reason: An episode grounded in powerful voices and moral questioning resonates with the raid’s raw activism and ethical urgency around voiceless animals.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 2013, a magnitude 6.6 earthquake struck Lushan County in Sichuan, China, toppling buildings and triggering landslides that killed at least 193 people. It hit a region still scarred by the devastating 2008 Wenchuan quake, testing the resilience of communities and the limits of seismic preparedness.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

queue.match_reason: The sudden, jarring force of an earthquake metaphorically mirrors the 'electric shock' jolt of this high-energy rock episode.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 2015, a bomb detonated beside a convoy delivering food to a United Nations compound in Garowe, the capital of Somalia's Puntland region, killing ten people. The attack highlighted the dangers faced by humanitarian workers trying to bring basic supplies into fragile, conflict-affected regions.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

queue.match_reason: A peaceful, travel-themed episode about moving through the world with awareness is a gentle counterpoint to a deadly attack on a convoy meant to deliver help.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 2007, an engineer at NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston, William Phillips, took a colleague hostage at gunpoint before killing him and then himself. The shooting pierced the image of NASA as a purely aspirational, spacebound institution, revealing the very earthly stresses and vulnerabilities inside its walls.

episode.title: Morning Drops #27 - Nomophobia
episode.description: Yasmin reflects on what happened when she recently lost her phone and why it felt freeing. A dive into the psychology of our relationship with smartphones: from nomophobia (the fear of being without a phone), to digital burnout and cognitive overload, to the surprising benefits of boredom and the brain's default mode network. An invitation to rethink how we connect and disconnect.

queue.match_reason: An episode probing modern psychological strain and hidden pressure sits alongside a tragic event where unseen stress exploded inside a high-tech workplace.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 2023, SpaceX’s Starship—the largest and most powerful rocket ever built—launched on its first test flight and dramatically disintegrated four minutes later. Though it ended in a fiery explosion, the attempt marked a bold step toward fully reusable super-heavy launch systems that could reshape access to space.

episode.title: Morning Drops #48 - Cosmic Monday
episode.description:
Cosmic Monday is a Morning Drops episode built around four Italian dance records, tracing how disco rhythms slowly became electronic language.
Gaia moves through four tracks like chapters: groove first, then machines, then that bright, futuristic feeling you only get when the bassline is doing the talking. It's not nostalgia, exactly. It's more like returning to a sound that still works — especially on mornings when you need a little extra energy, but you don't want anything aggressive.
Take it as a slow transmission. Let it carry you into the day.
songs: Giovanotti Mondani Meccanici - Don't Ask Me Why

queue.match_reason: A colossal rocket reaching for orbit and a "Cosmic" dance-floor transmission share a love of futurism, machines, and the thrill of ambitious launches—even when they don’t go perfectly.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1902, Pierre and Marie Curie’s refined radium chloride soon led to the eerie practice of adding radium to consumer products—from glow-in-the-dark watches to "health" tonics. The same element that helped treat cancers also caused horrific injuries to radium-dial painters, a stark reminder that scientific marvels can carry double edges.

episode.title: Morning Drops #47 - The Rise Of Autotune
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin explores the explosive rise of Auto-Tune, a tool that quietly changed the sound of music without asking for attention. From fragile, imperfect recordings to voices shaped with precision, this episode follows the moment when correction became standard and sound became controlled.

Along the way, we discover the curious story of how it was invented, how it moved from the background of studios to the center of debate, and how a few artists turned it into something expressive rather than hidden.

Listen slowly, and let us know what you think about Autotune.
songs: Chet Baker – I Fall in Love Too Easily

queue.match_reason: Radium and Auto-Tune are both revolutionary technologies that moved from subtle tools to cultural obsessions, sparking excitement, overuse, and ethical debate.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 2010, as the Deepwater Horizon rig burned, satellite images began to show a spreading rainbow sheen of oil across the Gulf—an enormous, unintended experiment in how far human-made pollution can travel on currents and wind. The disaster spurred new research into ocean dynamics, toxicology, and long-term ecosystem recovery.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause. A quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week.

queue.match_reason: The call for a 'slow planet' contrasts sharply with the high-speed environmental harm of the spill, inviting listeners to imagine gentler rhythms for Earth’s oceans.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 1972, while Apollo 16 astronauts explored the Moon, scientists on Earth pored over lunar samples from previous missions and realized the Moon is strikingly dry and ancient compared to Earth. The Apollo era didn’t just plant flags—it turned the Moon into a geological archive, helping us read the early history of the entire solar system.

episode.title: Evening Flows #01 - Yasmin - Crescendo in the Medina
episode.description: The first Evening Flow episode comes from Yasmin. It starts deep in the medina, slow, mysterious, textured, and grows into a liberating dance across sub-Saharan landscapes. A journey through ambient soundscapes, Arabic melodies, Afro-Latin rhythms, and funky highlife grooves.
songs: David Visan & Carlos Campos - Crystal Heart
Evan Mars - Ya Zareef Al Tool
Dele Sosimi & Medlar - You No Fit Touch Am (Medlar Remix)

queue.match_reason: Solar wind, lunar geology, and an episode that literally opens with "Solar Wind" weave a cosmic thread from medina rooftops to moon rocks.

queue.fact_text: On 20 April 2015 in Garowe, the United Nations convoy under attack was carrying something deceptively simple: food. Those sacks and crates represented complex global logistics and fragile trust—how the world’s institutions try, imperfectly, to move calories and care into places where conflict has stripped them away.

episode.title: Morning Drops #06 - Friend's Home
episode.description: Yasmin and Gaia are reunited under the same roof to spend Easter Monday morning together, exploring that heartwarming 'just like home' feeling that comes from being surrounded by beloved friends.

queue.match_reason: A convoy bringing food to a UN compound is, at its core, an attempt to create "home"—safety, nourishment, hope—in a dangerous place, mirroring the episode’s warmth of shared roofs and shared meals.

published.fact_text: On 20 April 1972, Apollo 16's Lunar Module Orion touched down in the Moon's Descartes Highlands, carrying astronauts John Young and Charles Duke. It was the penultimate crewed Moon landing, chosen specifically to explore what scientists thought might be ancient volcanic terrain—only to find it was mostly crushed highland crust, rewriting ideas about the Moon’s surface.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A literal shift into space and a mental 'space shift' align perfectly, with upbeat tracks echoing the bold optimism of a lunar landing.
